surface.name: Dynamics 365 suite router with a fully decomposed Business Central AL candidate
surface.selection_basis: Microsoft's current Dynamics 365 developer entry point describes a suite of business applications and routes each application family to different development resources. The suite ambiguity remains explicit, while the Business Central branch is recorded atomically because Microsoft's Get started with AL guide defines an end-to-end sandbox, tooling, project, publish, and HelloWorld path.

alternatives_considered.name: Business Central first AL app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Business Central is one Dynamics 365 application family with a distinct language and runtime.

alternatives_considered.name: Dataverse and model-driven app development
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route applies to Sales, Customer Service, Field Service, and related model-driven apps, not the entire suite.

alternatives_considered.name: Finance and operations app development
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Finance and Supply Chain development has its own environment, tools, deployment, and application model.

entry_point.developer_intent: Select a Dynamics 365 application and, if Business Central is selected, deploy and run Microsoft's generated HelloWorld AL extension on an online sandbox.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Dynamics 365 business application, tenant, entitlement, development environment, or extension model has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: No Dynamics 365 suite-wide first developer success is documented.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Unresolved suite-wide until a human selects a Dynamics 365 application and development job. In the fully decomposed Business Central candidate, the generated HelloWorld extension compiles, publishes, and runs on the online sandbox.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: For Business Central, Ctrl+F5 completes deployment, opens the online sandbox, and the generated HelloWorld sample runs. Other application families retain their own product-specific terminal signals.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The developer resources page is a product matrix, not a shared implementation tutorial, and it establishes no common terminal state.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Choosing a product still requires human intent. Once Business Central is selected, Microsoft's own onboarding guide identifies the first terminal state as a HelloWorld sample that compiles and runs after Ctrl+F5 deployment.

prerequisites.requirement: Select the Dynamics 365 business application and extension goal.
For the Business Central candidate, sign up for a Dynamics 365 Business Central online sandbox tenant.
Install Visual Studio Code and the AL Language extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central.
The Business Central user must be allowed to develop and publish extensions; Microsoft documents the EXTEN. MGT. - ADMIN permission set for that control.

primary_path.action: Open the Dynamics 365 developer resources matrix, identify the business application being extended, and select Business Central for this documented candidate.
details: The matrix separates Business Central, Finance, Supply Chain Management, Sales, Customer Service, Field Service, Marketing, model-driven apps, Commerce, and Human Resources.
The general docs separately offer product-specific trials and previews.
primary_path.input: Business job and target Dynamics 365 application.
primary_path.output: Business Central and its Get started with AL guide selected, or a deliberate route to another product family.
primary_path.success_signal: The Business Central AL tutorial is selected for steps 2 through 9.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If Business Central is not the intended product, stop this candidate and follow the selected product's separate guide.

primary_path.action: Use Microsoft's sandbox signup link to create or obtain a Dynamics 365 Business Central online sandbox tenant.
details: The AL guide lists a Business Central tenant as a prerequisite.
The credentials created or used here are required again when AL: Go! connects to the cloud sandbox.
primary_path.input: Microsoft identity and access to the linked Business Central sandbox signup.
primary_path.output: Online sandbox tenant and sign-in credentials.
primary_path.success_signal: The Business Central online sandbox is provisioned and accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Tenant provisioning, licensing, an existing organization policy, or missing administrator approval can delay access.

primary_path.action: Download and install Visual Studio Code.
details: Microsoft describes Visual Studio Code as the cross-platform editor used for AL coding and debugging.
primary_path.input: A supported local workstation and permission to install software.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Software-installation restrictions or an unsupported workstation can require administrator help.

primary_path.action: Install the AL Language extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central in Visual Studio Code.
details: The release version is the default documented route.
A prerelease version can be selected later, but it is not required for this first path.
primary_path.input: Visual Studio Code and access to the Visual Studio Marketplace.
primary_path.success_signal: AL commands, including AL: Go!, are available in Visual Studio Code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Marketplace access, extension policy, or a failed install can keep AL commands unavailable.

primary_path.action: Open User Settings with Ctrl+Shift+P and make the documented telemetry choice before creating the project.
details: Visual Studio Code telemetry is on by default in the guide's description.
To opt out, set telemetry.telemetryLevel to off in settings.json.
primary_path.input: A decision to retain the default telemetry behavior or opt out.
primary_path.output: Explicit telemetry configuration for the local editor.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer has retained the default or saved telemetry.telemetryLevel as off.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A read-only settings policy can prevent changing the local setting, but does not change the subsequent AL project steps.

primary_path.action: Run AL: Go!, choose a new empty folder and the Business Central version, then select Microsoft cloud sandbox as the server.
details: The documented keyboard sequence is Alt+A followed immediately by Alt+L.
AL: Go! generates the AL project and the app.json and launch.json configuration files.
primary_path.input: Empty local folder, selected target version, and cloud-sandbox server choice.
primary_path.output: Generated AL HelloWorld project with app.json and launch.json.
primary_path.success_signal: The generated project files appear in the selected folder.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: launch.json does not exist until AL: Go! runs; a non-empty or inaccessible folder can require a different location.

primary_path.action: Enter the Business Central credentials used for the sandbox signup when Visual Studio Code requests them.
details: Use AL: Clear credentials cache before retrying if cached credentials point at another environment.
primary_path.output: Authenticated AL connection to the selected cloud sandbox.
primary_path.success_signal: Visual Studio Code can connect and prepare the sandbox deployment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong cached credentials, tenant mismatch, missing EXTEN. MGT. - ADMIN permission, or an unavailable sandbox blocks publishing.

primary_path.action: Press Ctrl+F5 to build, publish, and run the generated extension on the online sandbox without debugging.
details: If the keyboard shortcut does not work, choose Run Without Debugging from Visual Studio Code's Run dropdown.
The operation packages and uploads the extension before the sandbox opens.
primary_path.input: Generated AL project, authenticated sandbox, and publish permission.
primary_path.output: Built and published extension running in Business Central.
primary_path.success_signal: The publish completes and Business Central opens the extension in the online sandbox.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Compile errors, duplicate Marketplace identifiers, expired credentials, permission failure, provisioning delay, or upload failure stop deployment.

primary_path.action: Verify that the generated HelloWorld sample has compiled and is running in the Business Central online sandbox.
details: Microsoft states that this terminal state means the HelloWorld example is up and running.
Editing a larger sample app is a next step and falls outside this first-success boundary.
primary_path.output: Running HelloWorld AL extension in Business Central.
primary_path.success_signal: The HelloWorld sample is visible and running in the opened sandbox session.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If Business Central does not open or the extension is absent, retry Run Without Debugging, clear the credentials cache if needed, and re-check sandbox permissions and launch.json.

branches.path: Continue through steps 2 through 9 to create the online sandbox, install Visual Studio Code and AL, make the telemetry choice, generate the cloud-sandbox project, authenticate, deploy with Ctrl+F5, and verify the running HelloWorld sample.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The result is Microsoft's generated HelloWorld AL extension compiled, published, and running in the Business Central online sandbox.

branches.condition: Sales, Customer Service, Field Service, Marketing, or a related model-driven app is selected.
branches.path: Use Dataverse and model-driven app developer resources with the appropriate Power Platform environment and solution model.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The result is a Dataverse or model-driven application change, not an AL or finance and operations package.

branches.condition: Finance or Supply Chain Management is selected.
branches.path: Use the finance and operations developer tools, UI development, and Lifecycle Services resources linked by Microsoft.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The required environment, build, packaging, and release lifecycle are finance and operations specific.

friction_gates.description: Dynamics 365 is a suite, so product and developer job must be selected before one path exists.
Product trials, previews, licenses, and entitlements are separate and product-specific.
Development environment and toolchain differ among AL, Dataverse and Power Platform, and finance and operations applications.
Tenant and environment roles required to customize or deploy depend on the selected application.
The AL extension must compile, package, upload, publish, and open in the online sandbox before HelloWorld can be verified.
The Business Central candidate ends only when Microsoft's generated HelloWorld sample is compiled and running, not when the sandbox or project is merely created.

sources.title: Developer resources for building and extending Dynamics 365 apps
Dynamics 365 documentation
sections_used: Copilot, agents, and generative AI features
evidence_supported: Lack of a shared developer implementation path

sources.title: Get started with AL
sections_used: Steps to set up a sandbox environment and Visual Studio Code
Controlling user access to develop and publish extensions
evidence_supported: Business Central tenant, Visual Studio Code, and AL extension prerequisites
AL: Go! folder, version, and Microsoft cloud sandbox selections
Ctrl+F5 deployment and Run Without Debugging recovery
Credential cache, duplicate identifier, and publish-permission recovery notes

uncertainties.question: Which Dynamics 365 application and extension job should define the first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes licensing, tenant and environment setup, language, tools, solution packaging, deployment, and visible success. This record fully resolves only the Business Central AL candidate.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Microsoft's official suite-level developer page explicitly routes each application family to different resources.

excluded_after_success: For non-Business-Central products, no common after-success scope is selected while product and extension goal remain unresolved.
For Business Central, editing the generated sample, larger AL objects, debugging, testing, packaging, and AppSource publication are excluded after HelloWorld runs.
Production solution lifecycle, AppSource publication, tenant governance, integrations, data migration, and product-specific administration are excluded.
